name: Alligator Cacciatora Banquet
recipeIngredient: 20 pounds Assorted green and red bell peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. Discard the head, feet, and tail of the alligators. Wash and cut into individual portions.
2. Use a large cooking pot that you can borrow, beg, or steal.
3. Heat vegetable oil in a medium skillet over low heat.
4. Add minced garlic to the hot oil and sauté until fragrant.
5. Add the alligator portions to the skillet, turning them often to cook evenly.
6. Once the alligators are nicely browned on all sides, add sliced zucchini, onions, and peppers.
7. Add canned tomatoes and bring to a boil.
8. Reduce heat to medium and simmer for 2 hours and 30 minutes.
9. Stir in salt, pepper, crushed red pepper, oregano, bay leaves, and parsley.
10. Simmer for an additional 5 minutes before adding sliced olives.
11. Serve with salad topped with your favorite Italian dressing and crisp bread.
New Orleans Cooks The Italian Way
Locovozzi & Mollicone (Note: This recipe reflects a real New Orleans Italian cooking tradition that is spicy like Cajun but truly Italian. If you don't have alligators in Milwaukee, consider using buffalo or other meats.)
